chief_complaint: A 45-year-old female presented with anterior chest pain, cough, and polyarthritis.
history: The patient reported a 2-week history of worsening anterior chest pain, accompanied by a cough and polyarthritis. She denied any recent trauma or infection. She had a history of relapsing polychondritis, for which she was on regular medication. She was a non-smoker and did not consume alcohol.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 90 bpm, RR 20 bpm. Physical examination revealed tenderness over the costochondral cartilage and joints.
labs: ECG: Normal. Bloods: ESR 80 mm/hr, CRP 120 mg/L. FDG-PET scan: Significant uptake in the costochondral cartilage, trachea, and joints.
